---
title: "Knuth's Hamiltonian Cycles, Solved by Claude"
description: "Donald Knuth's Hamiltonian cycle decomposition problem from The Art of Computer Programming was solved by Claude Opus 4.6."
author: "synesis"
date: "2026-03-08"
categories: [AI, mathematics, Mathematica, Claude, generative AI, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Cover image from the original LinkedIn post. ([Source](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_mathematica-math-genai-activity-7436552626042716160-qHiQ))](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

Donald Knuth found out that Hamiltonian cycle decomposition problem he came up with while writing The Art of Computer Programming was solved by Claude Opus 4.6 [[1]](#ref-1). Perhaps Mathematica will integrate with Claude now alongside ChatGPT?

(the reverse already exists)

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>[1] Knuth, Donald E. *Claude Cycles* (note). Stanford CS. <https://www-cs-faculty.stanford.edu/~knuth/papers/claude-cycles.pdf>

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_mathematica-math-genai-activity-7436552626042716160-qHiQ).*
